item.title: Small formatting nitpick
item.content:encoded: Is Blanchard's name supposed to be unredacted in line 3 of the first paragraph after "Discovery"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, like a cursed episode of Quantum Leap. It's a relatively simple idea, explained well, with some very complicated side-effects, that… Well it took me until the "I can see all my mistakes and can't avoid them" to realise just how debilitating and awful this SCP could be. The interview dialogue in particular flows very naturally, to my mind, I'm a big fan. And I can safely cross off "Viruses + time travel" from my list of potential ideas SCP hasn't covered. So far it's a very small list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, this is excellent. I just finished reading through up to part 9 of your tales, and this was crosslinked, and I love it. Most "surprise thaumiel!!1!" scips get old quick, considering it's a fairly common cliche, but this one works- sure, why wouldn't the Foundation need a way to quickly rewind time?
All this time stuff messes with my head. Reminds me of the Antimemetics Division, in a way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. Temporal stuff has always been interesting to me, but a temporal disease is just so strange it absolutely works. Furthermore, it somehow definitely holds my suspension of disbelief, probably in part due to excellent language choice. The interview definitely conveys the idea well, and that final addition is just amazing. A time drug? Brilliant. You now have opened up whole new pathways to explore in tales and the like.

Easy +1.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
This has been a few days coming, sorry for that HM.

+ Crit
- Crit

SCP-4583 is an anomalous, highly contagious strain of Influenza A (H1N1).

Noting that Type A Influenza is a classification of the flu virus but it contains multiple different strains of flu. H1N1 is a Type A Influenza virus, and was made popular as such from the 2009 Swine Flu scare, but there are others. If this is a swine flu, you may be better served calling it "H1N1 (commonly referred to as Swine Flu)" or something like that. Your info isn't wrong, but it wouldn't be how I would say it necessarily.

Every mistake I'll make for the rest of my life is sitting here in front of me and I can do nothing about them.

This is fucking horrifying, I love it.

Sufficient quantities of SCP-4583-T are released into the bloodstream by osmosis for its anomalous processes to manifest.

By definition, osmosis is referring to the diffusion of water. The proper term should probably be diffusion.

I loved the concept of Loopy for your last tale, and this is a really fucking cool way of discovering it. Easy +1, this is some good shit, Hammer.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to TheMightyMcB or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would've upvoted just for Billy Pilgrim's disease and I worried a bit when I opened up the back end and saw that there was more but it all ties in to itself without feeling like anything is extraneous. Even in the SCP format, it's tough to tell a time-traveling story well and I think this pulls it off rather nicely. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"RCT-Δt", very clever haha. This is a really interesting thaumiel, temporal anomalies are always cool and I love the idea of there being an anti-amnestic of sorts that let's people counteract them. Very well written too, love the interview log.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
time flu time flu

I'm glad to see it up! it's soo good. The story and idea really had me hooked the whole time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Research and Containment Team Δt. In includes retrieval agents, researchers, recon assets, and an MTF detachment for use in studying and containing threats to the collective past, present, and future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could someone remind me what RCT-Δt stands for? Something-change in time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is incredibly well written. Also, using 406 for something useful is something I never would have thought would happen.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
There's something funny about this bug. Everyone who has it starts getting things all out of order. But when the predictions they make start coming true? That's when things get really interesting.

Delta-t emblem is one of my own creation, based on the Foundation emblem we all know and love.
First Image is here under CC-By 2.0.
Second image source file is here under CC-By-SA 3.0, with my own alterations.

Special thanks to Doctor Fullham for the idea, MalyceGraves, and Mew-ltiverse for their help and feedback, and there's probably someone else I'm forgetting who had lots to say about it.

Enjoy!
